rulings.Case Title: FUND RECOVERY SERVICES, INC. VS. GARY C ALBERT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn To Compel Answers To Post Judgment Discovery; And For Award Of Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 6, 2013, Line 1: Plaintiff FUND RECOVERY SERVICES, INC.'S Motion to Compel Answers to Post Judgment Discovery; and for Award of Monetary Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Elizabeth Riles, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion Granted. Defendant is ordered to produce response within 15 days of this order. Sanctions of $390.00 against Defendant and Defendant's counsel are awarded to Plaintiff. Defendant and Defendant's counsel shall pay sanctions within 15 days of this order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to eriles@brhrlaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: SEIFER, MURKEN, DESPINA, JAMES & TEICHMAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File An Amended Petition To Compel Arb
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 6, 2013, Line 2, PETITIONER SEIFER, MURKEN, DESPINA, JAMES & TEICHMAN's MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File An Amended Petition To Compel arbitration. Petitioner's Motion to amend its petition to compel arbitration is denied. Petitioner does not cite authority that would permit the Court to transform a special proceeding (that is, a petition to compel arbitration) into a civil lawsuit. In making this order, the Court is not making any findings with regard to the statute of limitations or whether Petitioner has unreasonably delayed or caused prejudice to respondent. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT LOFTUS VS. CURTIS LEWIS
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Confirm Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 6, 2013, Line 5, PETITIONER ROBERT LOFTUS' Hearing On Petition To Confirm Arbitration. Hearing required. Petitioner is required to bring to Department 302 at 9:15 a.m. on the morning of the hearing a file stamped proof of service of the petition, showing compliance with CCP §1290.4(a) and/or (b), and a copy of the complete Petition. The Court does not have a courtesy copy of the 12 pages of attachments to the Petition and thus cannot determine whether the arbitration agreement provides the manner in which service of the petition shall be made. (See CCP §1290.4(a).) If the arbitration agreement does not provide the manner in which such service shall be made, the Court needs proof of timely service of summons. (CCP §1290.4(b)(1).) If service is not timely and in accordance with the statute, the motion is off calendar. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: STEVEN KAY VS. JOHN HANCOCK LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Responses And Further Responses To Defts' First Supplemental Requests For Production
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 6, 2013, Line 2: Defendants JOHN DEDOMINIC'S, TRADEMARK CAPITAL SERVICES'S, and M HOLDINGS SECURITIES INC.'S Motion to Compel Responses and Further Responses to Defendants' First Supplemental Requests for Production Pro Tem Judge Elizabeth Riles, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted, in part. Plaintiff did not comply with the Court's order of June 18 by providing courtesy copies of the Opposition papers pursuant to Local Rule 2.6B. Therefore, only Plaintiff's supplemental report was considered and not the opposition. Defendant's motion is partially granted as follows: Category 1 - all documents related to Steven Roth prior to his retention as a litigation consultant (May 2011); Category 2 - all communications with Lucinda Lee related only to the topics in Defendant's supplemental report; Category 3 - all documents from 2004 to the present; Category 4 - all documents from 2004 to the present; and Category 6 - all documents responsive to the request regarding the Son's Trust, should be produced. The Court further orders that documents identified in the privilege log which are Steven Roth communications prior to his retention as litigation consultant are to be produced and all Lucinda Lee communication regarding the three topic indentified in the Defendant's supplemental report are to be produced. The remainder or protected by attorney-client privilege and the attorney work produce doctrine and should not be produced. All documents are to be produced within 15 days of this order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to eriles@brhrlaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: RALPH BARBIERI VS. CUMULUS MEDIA INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave Of Court To Take Mental Examination Of Pltf
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 6, 2013, Line 4: Defendant CUMULUS MEDIA INC.'S Motion for Leave of Court to Take Mental Examination of Plaintiff Pro Tem Judge Elizabeth Riles, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is denied without prejudice. CCP sec. 2032.320(d) requires that an order for mental exam specify "person or persons who may perform the exam, as well as the time, place manner, DIAGNOSITC TESTS, PROCEDURES, condition, SCOPE and nature of the examination." (emphasis added). It is not clear from the order presented or the papers what or whether written tests will be administered. If so, they must be identified and who is administering them must be identified. The doctor suggests in his declaration that other tests MIGHT be performed as well. All such tests must be identified in the order as well as the scope of the examination. Therefore, the motion is denied and Defendant has leave to represent a motion and order that satisfies the CCP. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to eriles@brhrlaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: EXPANXION RECRUITING SERVICES, A CALIFORNIA VS. SOCIALCHORUS INC, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Socialchorus Inc, Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 6, 2013, Line 16, Defendant Socialchorus Inc.'s Motion For Summary Judgment. Defendant SocialChorus, Inc.'s Motion for Summary Judgment to Plaintiff expanXion's Complaint is denied. SocialChorus' motion for summary adjudication is granted as to the first cause of action for breach of contract, and denied as to the second cause of action for common counts. SocialChorus has shifted the burden by providing sufficient evidence that the parties did not enter into a written Fee Agreement for services on or about May 3, 2012 as alleged in the Complaint because the Fee Agreement was not signed, and SocialChorus had no intention of signing it. (See Declaration of Shove in Support of SocialChorus' Motion of Summary Judgment, or in the Alternative, Summary Adjudication ("Shove Declaration"), 4.) The burden shifted to Plaintiff to show that a triable issue of one or more material facts exists as to that cause of action. Plaintiff has failed to meets its burden. It did not submit any evidence with its opposition. Even taking into account the evidence submitted by Plaintiff in its separately filed Motion for Summary Judgment, Plaintiff has failed to show that the essential terms of the contract alleged in its Complaint were agreed upon through the email correspondence of May 3, 2012. As to the second cause of action for common counts, SocialChorus' has not shifted its burden. SocialChorus requested information and services from expanXion as to potential employees. (See Shove Declaration, Exh. B.) Even if the burden shifted, there is a triable issue of fact as to the services SocialChorus received from plaintiff. See evidence in opposition to Defendants' UMF 9 (Haber Decl., Ex. A (May 3, 2012, 1:44 p.m. email from Mr. Shove to Ms. Klic stating "ok, plse sked with sara[.] we need engineers as well.") Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: EXPANXION RECRUITING SERVICES, A CALIFORNIA VS. SOCIALCHORUS INC, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Expanxion Recruiting Services, A California Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 6, 2013, Line 16, Plaintiff Expanxion Recruiting Services' Motion For Summary Judgment. Plaintiff expanXion's Motion for Summary Judgment is denied. As for the first cause of action for breach of contract, Plaintiff has failed to establish that there is no material issue of fact with respect to this cause of action. There is a triable issue of fact whether Halogen Network and YouCast became SocialChorus, Inc. (UMF 4); and whether the parties did not enter into a written Fee Agreement for services on or about May 3, 2012 as alleged in the Complaint because the Fee Agreement was not signed, and SocialChorus had no intention of signing it. (UMF 10 and Shove Decl. in Support of Defendant's Opposition to Plaintiff's Motion for Summary Judgment, 4.). Plaintiff did not move in the alternative for summary adjudication of its cause of action for common counts. As such the Court will not address that cause of action on the merits. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: JOHN J. HUNTER VS. BETHEL AME CHURCH SAN FRANCISCO INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Ofmotion Andm Otion To Strike Pursuant To Cal Anti-Slapp Statute
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 6, 2013, Line 11, DEFENDANTS BETHEL AME CHURCH SAN FRANCISCO INC., LAWRENCE ACKER, RANDOLPH SCOTT, DENNIS WATTS, JAMES HOOKER, IDELL HILL & BOBBY SISK'S MOtion To Strike Pursuant To CalIFORNIA Anti-Slapp Statute. The motion to strike is granted as to the fourth of action for libel. As to the first prong of the anti-SLAPP analysis, Defendants have met their initial to show that the Resolution is protected activity under CCP §425.16(e)(3) or (e)(4). As to the second prong, Plaintiffs have not demonstrated a probability of prevailing on the merits. The statements in the resolution are subject to privilege. (Civ. Code §47(c). See Terry v. Davis Community Church (2005) 131 Cal.App.4th 1534, 1556 ( "'Ordinarily, the common interest of the members of a church in church matters is sufficient to give rise to a qualified privilege to communications between members on subjects relating to the church's interest.' [Citation.]").) Plaintiff has not demonstrated that the Resolution was made with malice or that the term "charges" was false. Further, the Resolution demanding the initiation of the conciliation process implicates the ministerial exception, which is another ground why Plaintiff cannot meet his burden on the second prong of the anti-SLAPP analysis. (Gunn v. Mariners Church, Inc. (2008) 167 Cal.App.4th 206, 214-15.) rulings.Case Title: AMY ESKIN VS. HERSH & HERSH PC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Seal Document Previously Filed With Court
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 6, 2013, Line 12, PLAINTIFF AMY ESKIN's motion To Seal Document Previously Filed With Court. Denied. Insufficient cause has been shown to seal documents that have been publicly available as an exhibit to the Complaint in this case since February 7, 2013, and then repeatedly re-filed in proposed and amended pleadings on May 6, 23, and 31, 2013. Further, the moving party has not shown that the document at issue, which is an exhibit prepared for this litigation, actually contains confidential information that is protected by unspecified confidentiality agreements in multiple other cases. Moving party can only state that that an order is necessary to prevent "possible disclosure" "in that the total amount of the settlement in each case could be estimated by making an assumption regarding the contingent fee percentages." (Marsh Decl. in support of Motion To File Document Under Seal, at 4)(emphasis added). Moving party has failed to show that there is an overriding interest that overcomes the right of public access to the record, that the overriding interest supports sealing the record, and that there is a substantial probability that the overriding interest will be prejudiced if the record is now sealed retroactively. (CRC 2.550(d).) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: NICHOLAS BLASGEN VS. REFRACTIVE DIALER, LLC*****CASE TRANSFERRED TO SACRAMENTO COUNTY*****
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Nicholas Blasgen'S Motion To Reconsider Court Order Granting Defendant'S Petition To Compel Mediation And Arbitration For Further Argument
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 6, 2013, Line 17, Plaintiff Nicholas Blasgen'S Motion To Reconsider Court Order Granting Defendant'S Petition To Compel Mediation And Arbitration For Further Argument. The Petition to Compel Mediation and Arbitration is denied. The Operating Agreement for Refractive Dialer, LLC, on which the petition is based, does not contain a binding agreement to arbitrate. (Decl. of Joshua Tillman In Support of Petition to Compel Mediation and Arbitration and Stay Action, filed May 1, 2013, 4, Ex. A.) Section VII (4) is entitled "Mediation and Arbitration of Disputes Among Members." The operative language states that "Any party may commence arbitration of the dispute by sending a written request for arbitration to all other parties to the dispute. The request shall state the nature of the dispute to be resolved by arbitration, and, if all parties to the dispute agree to arbitration, arbitration shall be commenced as soon as practical after such parties receive a copy of the written request." Id. Section VII (4) (emphasis added). The evidence shows that Plaintiff's earlier offers to arbitrate in late 2012 were never responded to by Defendant, and were withdrawn as rejected. (See Pokala Suppl. Decl. filed August 1, 2013, 4-6, and Exs. A-C.).) It was only after Plaintiff was no longer interested in arbitration and filed this lawsuit in late March 2013 that Defendant sought to arbitrate in late April. Plaintiff unequivocally refused to do so. (See Brothers Decl. filed May 1, 2013, 4, Ex. A (letters between counsel dated April 29, 2013.) The Court finds that all parties to the dispute do not agree to arbitrate, and the motion to compel is denied. The Court has no authority to compel mediation. Requests for attorneys fees are denied. The Court confirms that the previous order announced in Court on June 5, 2013 granting the petition to compel mediation is vacated.
